title: Threading The List
prompt: A dragon guards the hatch. Rewrite the appends away to earn the key.
help: This room is about rewriting with the two append laws. `forward(List(A), lhs, rhs, eq, motive)` replaces `lhs` with `rhs`, the motive being the goal with `_` at the spot. Shortcut: `by rewrite(app_cons)?;` (or `by rewrite(app_nil)?;`) infers the arguments and suggests the full `by forward(…) then …;` step. Finish both lemmas. (1) app_left_nil is one axiom: `by app_nil(A, ys);`. (2) app_singleton: rewrite the outer app with app_cons (motive `_ = cons(A, x, ys)`) to reach `⊢ cons(A, x, app(A, nil(A), ys)) = cons(A, x, ys);`, then rewrite the inner `app(A, nil(A), ys)` with app_nil (motive `cons(A, x, _) = cons(A, x, ys)`) and close with `by refl(List(A), cons(A, x, ys));`.
